en: What's the matter?
I need to talk to you. About what?
They'd rather we didn't keep the name.
ko: 그들은 우리가 이름을 유지하지 않기를 원해.

en: Mountbatten.
Who's they?
Cabinet.
It's none of their business.
I think you'll find it's very much their business.
You're my wife. Taking my name is the law.
ko: 넌 내 아내야. 내 이름을 쓰는 건 법이야.

en: It's the custom, not the law.
A custom practiced so universally, it might as well be the law. You can't do this.
ko: 그렇게 보편적으로 행해지는 관습은 법과 다름없어. 이건 할 수 없어.

en: Am I to be the only man in the country
ko: 내가 나라에서 유일하게 아내와 자녀가 내 이름을 쓰지 않는 남자가 되어야 해?

en: whose wife and children don't take his name?
ko: 아내와 자녀가 내 이름을 쓰지 않는 남자가 되어야 해?

en: You can't do this to Dickie. It will devastate him. You know that.
ko: 이렇게 하면 딕키에게 큰 상처가 될 거야. 너도 알잖아.

en: You know how important it is to him.
ko: 그에게 얼마나 중요한 일인지 알고 있어.

en: I've told him the Royal House Mountbatten is in the bag.
ko: 나는 그에게 로열 하우스 마운트배튼이 확정됐다고 말했어.

en: That was a mistake. It's not.
The name has to be Windsor.
For stability. There's more.
Clarence House.
Our home. What about it?
We have to give it up.
